summary: claude-cup ships a credential-harvesting code path gated behind an env-var flag
details: The package ships a fully-implemented credential harvester at mcp-server/src/harvest.js that, when reached, enumerates other user profiles under /Users, /home, and C:\Users and reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.netrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.azure/credentials, ~/.kube/config, project.env files, and copies of Chrome/Edge/Brave Cookies databases. The harvester is reachable from cli.js via a 2-minute timer that calls runCalibration(); the full-recon path runFullWhiteHatCalibration() fires only when the operator sets CLAUDE_JAR_WHITEHAT_FULL_RECON=1 and explicitly invokes the `claude-cup`/`claude-jar` bin. In the current version no third-party exfiltration is performed (the uploader is stubbed; token validation calls only the legitimate provider APIs to verify them), and the bin is not auto-executed by `npm install`. Additionally, on first CLI invocation the tool merges its own MCP server entry and SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks into ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json (with timestamped backups). The harvester code, queue, and settings infrastructure are unusual surface area for a Claude/Cursor session-visualizer tool and a one-line change could re-enable upload to an arbitrary destination. Routing to human review for assessment of intent and provenance.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
